biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — violations in ~5 of 12 substantive tool-using units (~42%): 41a73142-0 (ls-la compound Bash at session entry fails success=false); 41a73142-1 (ls-la+find compound Bash); afe26d7d-0 (cat heredoc Bash for build.py instead of Write, fails success=false before Write fallback adopted); db668075-2 (cat package.json|head-80 in compound Bash instead of Read); db668075-7 (cat heredoc in compound Bash for /tmp/memprobe.mjs fails before Write fallback). Rate ~42% — regression from 2026-06-17 low of 30% back into the 40-44% band; marginal-improvement trajectory from prior cycle not sustained.

compound_command_overreach:RECURRING:STABLE — 41a73142-0: session-entry compound (claude-ipc+ls+wal check) fails success=false before decomposition; afe26d7d-0: cat heredoc+python3 compound fails success=false before Write fallback; db668075-7: compound Bash with heredoc write+curl+node fails success=false. Decompose-only-after-failure sequence structurally identical across all prior cycles; no pre-decomposition behavior observed in any confirmed instance across full calibration history.

hook_bypass_for_convenience:RECURRING:STABLE — 41a73142-1: touch ~/.claude/.no-dup-symbol-guard executed after three consecutive Edit failures on src/render.ts; guard restored via trash only after all edits complete. Fifth confirmed instance. Trigger condition (consecutive Edit failures on same target file), mechanism (touch .no-*-guard), and late-restoration behavior structurally identical across all five instances; correct response (re-read file, identify conflict, resolve without disabling guard) has never been attempted in any confirmed instance.

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:STABLE — 41a73142-1: three or more consecutive Edit failures on src/render.ts before hook bypass adopted as exit rather than re-reading target file. Single-unit occurrence consistent with prior-cycle pattern; structurally identical to all prior instances.

git_add_A_instead_of_specific_files:RECURRING — 41a73142-34: git add -A used in commit pipeline, violating rules/git.md ('prefer adding specific files by name rather than git add -A or git add .'). Was flagged NEW in 2026-06-18 cycle; now second confirmed instance. Low severity; no user correction observed in either instance but violation is unambiguous.

sed_probe_edit_via_bash:RECURRING — 41a73142-34: sed -e substitution on probe-imgdim.js piped to probe-imgdim2.js to generate probe variant instead of Write tool. Was flagged NEW in 2026-06-18 cycle; now second confirmed instance. Shell-for-file-editing sub-variant of shell_tool_preference_violations occurring specifically in probe/test variant generation context.
recommendations: hook_bypass_for_convenience: five confirmed instances, zero re-read-and-resolve attempts, advisory path fully exhausted. A PreToolUse hook intercepting touch commands matching .no-*-guard and injecting a directive to re-read the failing target file first is the only unexhausted enforcement mechanism; implement or route to proposal backlog.

shell_tool_preference_violations: rate oscillated 30-44% across four cycles with no structural downward trend; per-session rule restatement demonstrably insufficient. A PreToolUse hook detecting cat <file>|head, sed -n, and ls -la patterns targeting source files and surfacing the Read-tool alternative would move enforcement to the tool layer.

compound_command_overreach at session entry: structurally identical failure in every observed session-start. A UserPromptSubmit hook for session-start that fires a decomposition reminder when the prior turn contained a compound failure would catch this before the wasted first tool call.

git_add_A_instead_of_specific_files: graduated from NEW to RECURRING; a PreToolUse hook blocking git add -A or git add . in Bash calls and requiring explicit file paths would enforce rules/git.md mechanically at minimal cost.
